mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, which expands profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and inventory restocking, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to generate positive 5-day returns during periods of simultaneous monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income and consumer expenditure, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns across all business cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500 thousand units indicate a severe credit contraction and collapsing construction sector demand, which drags down equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of quarters where housing starts drop below the 500 thousand threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising industrial production create an optimal liquidity and output environment, which amplifies equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent regardless of INDPRO trends.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent coincides with negative 60-day equity returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.
